title: Dana Siddle
content:
Partner | Health Industry Group Co-Leader
People.ContactEmail dsiddle@mccarthy.ca
- New South Wales, 2006
- British Columbia, 2010
- University of New South Wales
Dana Siddle is a partner in the Vancouver office. She is a member of our Technology Law Group, and Co-Leader of our Health Industry Group. Known for her in-depth experience and practical approach, Dana advises on complex commercial transactions and technology procurements across a broad spectrum of industries.
Dana also advises on data protection, privacy, artificial intelligence, and other regulatory matters in commercial transactions.
In the health industry, Dana supports clients with projects involving healthtech, medtech, health data management, health benefits, clinical trials, and procurements by hospitals and health authorities.

### Dana’s recent experience includes:

#### Procurement and Outsourcing
- Acting for a health authority and other public bodies in connection with the procurement and implementation of leading cloud-based human capital management solutions.
- Acting for a transportation authority in connection with a procurement and implementation of an information security SaaS solution for identity governance and administration (IGA).
- Acting for a public body in connection with a procurement and implementation of a SaaS solution for product information management.
- Advising on freedom of information (FOI) applications.
- Advising leading financial services institutions on an agreement for mortgage underwriting services, and numerous technology services engagements.
- Acting for successful proponents under competitive procurement processes in the negotiation of long-term services agreements for healthcare and technology services in the public healthcare system, including in relation to MSP claims administration, call center services, and laboratory services.
- Acting for a healthtech organization in connection with its preparation and negotiation of a joint venture and collaboration agreement relating to the development of technology for health records interoperability.
- Advising on clinic/facility usage agreements.
- Acting for a public body and a private health benefits provider in connection with the procurement of medical imaging SaaS solutions.
- Acting for a medtech manufacturer of medication management solutions in connection with the review and negotiation of hospital procurement agreements.
- Acting for an innovative home health care services provider in connection with technology services agreements, and privacy and data protection issues.
- Acting for an industry organization in connection with the establishment of a COVID-19 rapid antigen screening program.

#### Complex Commercial Transactions
- Acting for major retailers in connection with a wide variety of complex commercial transactions, including for a co-branded credit card, payment processing, home renovation services, product packaging, SaaS subscriptions and other IT services.
- Acting for property developers in connection with the preparation and negotiation of contracts for electric vehicle charging, valet services, digital signage, digital immersive experiences, and mobile app development.
- Negotiating agreements for data centre co-location services, and telecommunication, internet and satellite services.
- Drafting and negotiating numerous agreements for cloud services, SaaS services, hardware, software, support and maintenance services, reseller services, distribution rights, co-development and collaboration projects, intellectual property licensing, and staff augmentation services.
Prior to joining McCarthy Tétrault, Dana was a senior in-house lawyer for a Fortune 500 consumer electronics retailer. She led contractual negotiations and provided legal advisory services for a high volume of technology procurements, IT and e-commerce projects, consumer-facing programs, and regulatory compliance. She regularly draws upon this valuable experience and insight to support in-house legal and business teams with their most complex issues and strategic goals.
Dana is an engaged member of the community, and has volunteered with the North Vancouver Football Club, and as a director and board secretary of Easter Seals (BC/Yukon).
Previously, Dana practiced at other leading national law firms in Canada and Australia.
